briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To provide image manipulation utilities for the document processing pipeline: cropping, rotation, pixel frequency analysis, bounding box extraction, and text area detection.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Image cropping, rotation, edge detection, bounding box operations, image concatenation, text area isolation. \par \b Input\/ \: numpy arrays, PIL Images, BBox objects. \par \b Output\/ \: numpy arrays, PIL Images, BBox objects. \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(8): PIL, numpy, opencv, scipy] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: rotate_image always returns a non-cropped image with expanded dimensions.
crop_image_text always returns a valid BBox even on empty edges.
get_concat_v returns a single image if input list has length 1. \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why expand image bounds during rotation? A: Avoids information loss from corner clipping, which is critical for OCR quality. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 – Added SEMANTIC TEMPLATE markup and LDD logging] \par \b Module Map\/ \:

FUNC 6[Highest pixel frequency color] => get_highest_pixel_frequency FUNC 7[BBox crop + resize] => get_bbox_from_image FUNC 8[Image rotation with bound expansion] => rotate_image FUNC 7[Text area crop via Canny edges] => crop_image_text FUNC 5[Rectangle overlay on image] => draw_rectangle FUNC 5[Vertical image concatenation] => get_concat_v \par \b Use Cases\/ \:

listitem.para: [rotate_image]: Preprocessor → DeskewImage → Rotated image ready for OCR

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To find the most frequent pixel intensity in a grayscale image, avoiding black (0) and defaulting to white (255) for background detection.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Uses\/ \: numpy \par \b I/O\/ \: np.ndarray -> int \par \b Complexity\/ \: 3

memberdef.argsstring: (Image.Image image, BBox bbox, Tuple[int, int] resize=(300, 15))
memberdef.name: get_bbox_from_image

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To extract a sub-region from an image by bounding box, with optional resizing for uniform downstream processing.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Uses\/ \: PIL.Image \par \b I/O\/ \: (Image.Image, BBox, Tuple[int,int]) -> Image.Image \par \b Complexity\/ \: 4

memberdef.argsstring: (np.ndarray image, float angle, Tuple[int, int, int] color_bound=(255, 255, 255))
memberdef.name: rotate_image

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To rotate an image by a given angle with automatic bound expansion, preventing corner cropping and preserving all content for OCR.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Uses\/ \: opencv \par \b I/O\/ \: (np.ndarray, float, Tuple[int,int,int]) -> np.ndarray \par \b Complexity\/ \: 6

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To detect the bounding box of text content in an image using Canny edge detection and maximum filtering, cropping out empty margins.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Uses\/ \: opencv, scipy.ndimage.maximum_filter \par \b I/O\/ \: np.ndarray -> BBox \par \b Complexity\/ \: 7

memberdef.argsstring: (Image.Image image, int x_top_left, int y_top_left, int width, int height, Tuple[int, int, int] color=(0, 0, 0))
memberdef.name: draw_rectangle

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To draw a colored rectangle outline on an image, used for visualizing detected bounding boxes in debug/demo outputs.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Uses\/ \: PIL.ImageDraw, deepcopy \par \b I/O\/ \: (Image.Image, int, int, int, int, Tuple[int,int,int]) -> np.ndarray \par \b Complexity\/ \: 3

briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To vertically concatenate a list of PIL images into a single combined image, preserving the maximum width.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Uses\/ \: PIL.Image \par \b I/O\/ \: List[Image.Image] -> Image.Image \par \b Complexity\/ \: 3
